learningObjectives.title: Understand end-to-end planning lifecycle
learningObjectives.description: Explain full planning flow from ERP demand to freight settlement.

learningObjectives.title: Understand decision points
learningObjectives.description: Identify key planning decisions across consolidation, optimization and assignment stages.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate ERP and TM responsibilities
learningObjectives.description: Clarify business document ownership and execution scope between ERP and SAP TM.

sections.content: The end-to-end transportation planning process in SAP TM transforms transportation demand into executable shipments. Starting from ERP business documents, SAP TM creates Freight Units, performs optimization, selects carriers and resources, generates Freight Orders and finally releases them for transportation execution.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Transportation Planning is a complete business process, not a single activity. Every step depends on accurate master data and planning rules.

items: Which transportation mode should be used?
Which carrier offers the best service?

sections.content: A manufacturing company receives 500 customer deliveries every day. SAP TM automatically creates Freight Units from outbound deliveries, consolidates compatible shipments, optimizes routes, assigns approved carriers and available trucks, generates Freight Orders and releases them for execution. After delivery, freight charges are calculated and settled with the carrier.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Implement automatic planning for standard transportation scenarios and reserve manual planning for exceptions. This provides the best balance between efficiency and business control.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A multinational FMCG company processes over 30,000 Freight Units daily. SAP TM automatically creates optimized Freight Orders overnight, assigns preferred carriers, validates capacities and releases shipments before business hours, reducing transportation planning effort by over 70%.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Be able to explain the complete SAP TM planning flow from Sales Order -> Delivery -> Freight Unit -> Transportation Cockpit -> Optimizer -> Carrier Selection -> Resource Assignment -> Freight Order -> Execution -> Freight Settlement.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Many beginners believe Freight Orders are created directly from Sales Orders. In SAP TM, Freight Orders are created only after Freight Units have been built and transportation planning has been completed.

items: Transportation Planning starts with Freight Units.
The optimizer creates efficient transportation plans.
Carrier Selection occurs before Resource Assignment.
Freight Orders are the primary execution documents.
Transportation Planning ends with Freight Settlement after execution.
Accurate master data is essential for every planning step.
